title: Canonical Serialization Test Vectors
description: Canonical serialization test vectors verifying the rules from wire-format.md Section 3.7. In v2, canonical serialization is applied to the full DecryptedPayload (including the wrapper fields: deviceSequence, entityType, entityId, operation, clientTimestamp, protocolVersion, data). Keys are sorted lexicographically (Unicode code point order) at every nesting level including inside the data object. Output is compact (no whitespace). Null/absent fields are omitted. All SHA-256 hashes are computed over the UTF-8 byte representation of the canonical JSON string.

serializationRules.rule1: Object keys MUST be sorted lexicographically (Unicode code point order) at every nesting level, including inside the 'data' object
serializationRules.rule2: No whitespace between tokens (compact form: separators are ',' and ':')
serializationRules.rule3: Null/absent optional fields MUST be omitted (not serialized as null)
serializationRules.rule4: The resulting UTF-8 byte sequence is the canonical form
serializationRules.rule5: UUIDs are lowercase hex with hyphens
serializationRules.rule6: Timestamps use ISO 8601 with exactly 3 fractional digits and Z suffix
serializationRules.rule7: Integer values have no fractional parts
serializationRules.rule8: payerResponsibilityRatio is a JSON number with at most 4 decimal places
serializationRules.rule9: v2: Canonical form includes the full DecryptedPayload wrapper (clientTimestamp, data, deviceSequence, entityId, entityType, operation, protocolVersion), not just the entity-specific data

testCases.description: CalendarEvent CREATE with minimal fields (no optional fields). Verifies that optional fields (time, location, notes) are omitted, not serialized as null.
notes: Optional data fields (time, location, notes) are NOT present in the output
Wrapper keys sorted: clientTimestamp < data < deviceSequence < entityId < entityType < operation < protocolVersion
Data keys sorted: childId < date < eventId < title

testCases.description: CalendarEvent CREATE with ALL optional fields present. Verifies that optional fields are included when present and sorted among other keys.
testCases.expectedCanonicalJson: {"clientTimestamp":"2026-03-16T12:00:00.000Z","data":{"childId":"c1d2e3f4-5678-9abc-def0-123456789012","date":"2026-04-10","eventId":"11223344-5566-7788-99aa-bbccddeeff00","location":"Grundschule am Park, Room 201","notes":"Bring last semester's report card.","time":"15:30:00","title":"Parent-teacher conference"},"deviceSequence":1,"entityId":"11223344-5566-7788-99aa-bbccddeeff00","entityType":"CalendarEvent","operation":"CREATE","protocolVersion":2}
notes: Inside data: location sorts between eventId and notes
Inside data: notes sorts between location and time
Inside data: time sorts between notes and title

testCases.description: Expense CREATE with numeric precision edge case. payerResponsibilityRatio of 0.6 must serialize as '0.6' (not '0.60' or '0.6000').
notes: data.amountCents is an integer (15099 not 15099.0)
data.payerResponsibilityRatio is 0.6 (not 0.60 or 0.6000)
data.currencyCode is uppercase 3-letter ISO 4217

testCases.description: CalendarEvent DELETE with very minimal data fields. Smallest possible DecryptedPayload.
notes: Minimal data object with only eventId
7 wrapper fields + 1 data field = 8 total fields
operation is DELETE

testCases.description: ExpenseStatus UPDATE WITH optional note field. Verifies that the note field is included when present.

data.note: Amount seems too high for a standard visit. Can you share the invoice?

testCases.expectedCanonicalJson: {"clientTimestamp":"2026-03-19T09:00:00.000Z","data":{"expenseId":"a1234567-b890-cdef-1234-567890abcdef","note":"Amount seems too high for a standard visit. Can you share the invoice?","responderId":"e2f3a4b5-6789-abcd-ef01-bbbbbbbbbbbb","status":"DISPUTED"},"deviceSequence":1,"entityId":"a1234567-b890-cdef-1234-567890abcdef","entityType":"ExpenseStatus","operation":"UPDATE","protocolVersion":2}
notes: data.note field is present and sorts between expenseId and responderId
data.status is an enum string in UPPERCASE

testCases.description: ExpenseStatus UPDATE WITHOUT optional note field. Verifies that the note field is omitted (not null).
notes: data.note field is absent (NOT serialized as 'note':null)
Compare with CS-05: same payload type but note is omitted, producing a different hash
data has 3 fields vs 4 fields in CS-05

testCases.description: Expense CREATE with receipt blob fields. Verifies that receiptBlobId and receiptDecryptionKey are included and sorted correctly inside data.
notes: data.receiptBlobId sorts after data.payerResponsibilityRatio and before data.receiptDecryptionKey
data.receiptDecryptionKey is a base64 string (not raw bytes in the JSON)
Both receipt fields are present together (dependentRequired)

antiPatterns.description: Examples of INCORRECT canonical serialization that implementations MUST NOT produce

examples.error: Null field included instead of omitted
examples.rule: wire-format.md Section 3.3: Fields with null values MUST be omitted

examples.error: Keys not sorted at top level
examples.rule: wire-format.md Section 3.7: Object keys MUST be sorted lexicographically
wire-format.md Section 3.7: No whitespace between tokens (compact form)
wire-format.md Section 3.6: Integer values without fractional parts; ratio with minimal precision

examples.error: Timestamp missing milliseconds or Z suffix
examples.rule: wire-format.md Section 3.4: Instant values MUST include exactly 3 fractional digits
wire-format.md Section 3.5: UUID lowercase hex with hyphens

examples.error: Keys not sorted inside nested data object
examples.rule: wire-format.md Section 3.7: Keys sorted at EVERY nesting level including inside data
